data.title: As a team lead, I can validate the updated onboarding experience by comparing before/after runs on Nava template projects
data.description:
## Description

**What**: Conduct a before/after validation walkthrough comparing current onboarding vs. updated onboarding on projects initialized with Nava project templates
**Why**: Ensures the UX improvements actually work end-to-end and integration prompts are discoverable
**Scope**:
- IN: Run current onboarding on a Nava template project, run updated onboarding on an equivalent fresh template project, verify 5 binary pass/fail criteria
- OUT: Automated regression testing, performance benchmarking

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the updated onboarding completes without errors on a fresh Nava template project
  ← Satisfies: "updated onboarding completes without errors on a fresh template project"
- When this story is complete, all three integration prompts (Jira, Figma, Confluence) appear in the onboarding dialogue
  ← Satisfies: "all three integration prompts appear in the dialogue"
- When this story is complete, the .claude/scripts/dso shim is present and executable after completion
  ← Satisfies: "the shim is present and executable after completion"
- When this story is complete, dso-config.conf contains no credential values (API tokens, PATs)
  ← Satisfies: "dso-config.conf contains no credential values"
- When this story is complete, the phase counter displays at the start of each phase without gaps or jumps
  ← Satisfies: "phase counter displays at the start of each phase without gaps or jumps"

## Considerations

- [Testing] This is a manual validation story — all criteria are binary pass/fail

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
